role: Speaker of Parliament (2008–2020); Former chief nuclear negotiator (d. 2026)
bio: Ali Larijani was the longest-serving speaker in the history of the Islamic Republic, holding the position from 2008 to 2020. Before entering politics, he served as head of [IRIB](/orgs/irib) (1994–2004), appointed by [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) as a hardline counterweight to the reformist press during the Khatami era. He subsequently served as Iran's chief nuclear negotiator (2005–2007) and secretary of the [Supreme National Security Council](/orgs/supreme-national-security-council). He was disqualified from the 2021 presidential election by the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council). His brother [Sadegh Larijani](/people/sadegh-larijani) served as head of the judiciary. He was killed on 17 March 2026 in an [Israeli airstrike on Tehran](/events/larijani-assassination-2026), along with his son Morteza Larijani.
editorial_notes: Born in Najaf to an Iranian clerical family. Disqualified from 2021 presidential election. Killed 17 March 2026 in Israeli airstrike on Tehran.
